advancedTradingTips:
The set stop-profit stop loss will be automatically placed after the buy transaction:
  • 1. When the rising conditions are met, it will automatically sell according to the set ratio (take profit)
  • 2. When the falling conditions are met, it will automatically sell and stop loss
  • 3. Multi-stage strategies are triggered in order, and those that are met will be executed first.
  • 4. The total selling ratio cannot exceed 100%. The order can be cancelled manually on the chart
autoSellHalfTips: Double Sell 50% means doubling the asset value to recover the initial investment.
devSellTips: DEV Sell refers to the automatic sell of a corresponding proportion when the token creator (DEV) sells a certain percentage.
trailingStopTips: Trailing stop refers to automatically selling a corresponding proportion of tokens when the token retraces a certain percentage from a certain high point.
listingOnDexTips: Listing on Dex refers to the automatic selling ratio after the tokens are moved to the dex swap(amm) with full bonding.
takeProfitAndStopLossTips: You can set multiple take-profit or stop-loss rules. Once activated, new purchased tokens will follow these rules.

step1Tip1: Currently, Ave is not available on the Chinese mainland App Store. To download the Ave app, you’ll need to switch to a supported Apple ID region.
step2Tip1: Switch Apple ID in the App Store.
step2Tip2: the App Store, tap the avatar icon in the top right corner, and sign out.
step2Tip3: Sign in with an Apple ID registered in a supported region.
step2Tip4: If login fails, it might be due to device restrictions or security settings. Try logging in from the device settings directly. If the ID is locked, you may need to unlock it in the Apple ID settings.
step3Tip1: Once signed in, the App Store will automatically switch regions. Search for “Ave” in the App Store and tap Get to install.
step3Tip2: After installation, return to the home screen and open the Ave app.

safeDsc: To ensure the safety of your account and funds, please complete the following two safety certifications immediately.
emailVerificationDsc: Bind your email address to protect your account and transactions
googleVerificationDsc: Use Google Verification Code to protect your account and funds
resetGoogleAuthDsc:
The unbinding and reset of Google Verifier currently needs to be manually reviewed. To ensure the security of your account, please go to the official Telegram group to submit an application. 
Click the button below to jump to the Telegram group for processing.
bindGoogleAuthReadmeP1:
Google Authenticator is a time-based one-time verification code tool used to enhance account security. 
Users need to install the Google Verifier App on their mobile phones and generate a set of verification codes every 30 seconds after binding the account.
bindGoogleAuthReadmeT2: Why do I need Google Verifier?
bindGoogleAuthReadmeP2:
In order to ensure the security of Bot wallet assets, the platform will require users to complete secondary verification when performing withdrawals and transfers. 
The verification steps include: Enter the bound Google Verifier dynamic verification code.
bindGoogleAuthStep1P1: iOS user login App Store Search "Authenticator" to download
bindGoogleAuthStep1P2: Android users log in to the app store or use their mobile browser to search for "Google Verifier" to download
bindGoogleAuthStep2P1: Open Google Authenticator, click the number in the lower right corner and click to scan the QR code (please scan again after refreshing the page). Be sure to save the key, it will not be retrieved if it is lost.
bindGoogleAuthStep3P1: Open Google Verifier, use the key or scan the QR code to add verification, and enter the 6-bit verification code on the Verifier after completion

2faP1: Enabling two-factor authentication (2FA) will protect your account and funds from security, and it is expected to take effect 12 hours after the initial binding, and withdrawals are prohibited during this period.
2faT2: Completed binding and waited for effect

question: Frequently Asked Questions
question1Title: 1. What is the rent for the Solana account?
question1Content: On the Solana network, each token and NFT has independent accounts that require a certain amount of SOL as rent to be used normally.
question2Content:
To close your Solana account, clear all remaining tokens or NFTs in your account first. 
Once closed, the SOL used as the rent will be returned to you.
question3Title: 3. How to learn more about Solana?
question3Content: Please check the official Solana document

allPairsTips: Aggregation mode: Displays the accumulated results of the number and initial value of the token in all pools, which is different from the data of a single pool.
blackListPlaceHolder: Enter developer address, token contract, and keywords
overLimit: Over limit, please confirm if it is filled incorrectly
cancelORPauseTip: If there is a long-term shortage of funds and no trading, the system will assume that the account is inactive and automatically suspend strategy execution
sellOutTip: EVM follow-up selling issue: In EVM follow-up, when the target is to sell 50% of the position, due to delayed synchronization of position data, the system cannot determine whether to clear the position or sell half. When automatic follow-up and proportional selling occur, the position will be cleared directly
highStrategyTip: Obtain short-term spreads with higher trading frequency. Suitable for users who can withstand fluctuations, pursue efficiency, and leverage returns. Multiple signals, fast changes, synchronous amplification of returns and risks
lowStrategyTip: Lock in medium to long-term trends through low-frequency trading. Slow pace, high fault tolerance, and greater emphasis on stable returns and risk control. Suitable for users who prefer stability and have low resistance to fluctuations.
fixedAmountTip:
Purchase at a fixed amount per order, regardless of the actual purchase amount at the target address.
Example: Set 5 SOL, buy 2/20 SOL at the target address, and you both buy 5 SOL.
maxCopyTradeTip:
The system will first calculate the「original purchase amount」 according to the ratio you set. If the amount exceeds the maximum purchase amount you set, only buy the maximum value to avoid buying too much at once.
Example: The target address bought 20 SOLs, and you set a 50% order fulfillment ratio. According to the ratio, you were supposed to buy 10 SOLs; But you only want to buy 5 SOLs at most once, so this time you will actually only buy 5 SOLs.
buyRatioTip:
Map the proportion of the target address's current purchase to its total position to your position purchase in equal proportion.
Example: The target address has a storage space of 100 SOL, and this time I am buying 10 SOL (10%); Your position is 10 SOL → Buy 1 SOL.
resetTips: Reset will hide your custom fields and return to the default display. The filtering conditions remain unchanged. Are you sure to reset?
preferedTips: This indicator shows the distribution of the market cap of the tokens you often buy and the corresponding purchase frequency, helping you understand your trading preferences.
takeProfitAndStopLoss4: Break-Even on Double + All-Out on Triple + 50% SL
takeProfitAndStopLoss6: All-Out on 40% TP + Trailing TP/SL (60% Drawdown)
takeProfitAndStopLoss5: All-Out on Tenfold + Trailing TP/SL (40% Drawdown)
takeProfitAndStopLossTips1: Double out means that when the position profit reaches 100%, the system automatically closes all positions.
takeProfitAndStopLossTips2: Double the capital out means to close the position and pay out 50% of the capital when the profit reaches 100%; stop loss at half means to automatically close the whole position when the loss reaches 50%.
takeProfitAndStopLossTips3: Double all-out means that all positions will be closed when the profit reaches 100%; half-stop loss means that all positions will be automatically closed when the loss reaches 50%.
takeProfitAndStopLossTips4: Double out (100% profit and 50% of the position is closed); Triple out (the remaining position is 300% and the profit is all closed); Half stop loss (50% of the loss is all closed).
takeProfitAndStopLossTips5: 10 times take profit (1000% profit all-even); moving take profit means that all positions will be automatically closed when the price retraces 40% from the highest point.
takeProfitAndStopLossTips6: 40% take profit (40% profit closing); trailing take profit means that all positions will be automatically closed when the price retraces 60% from the highest point.
notUpdateExistRemark: Remarks on the wallet will not be modified.
notUpdateExistRemarkTip: Note: If unchecked, this will overwrite any existing remarks on the wallet. Please choose carefully.
validityPeriod3: GTT（Always Valid Until Expiration）: This order will remain valid until it is fully executed or canceled.
immediateOrCancel2: IOC（Immediate or Cancel）: The order must be executed immediately or partially, and the remaining part will be canceled.
longPnl: Closed P&L = Close Value - Open Value - Fee + Funding Fee
shortPnl: Closed P&L = Open Value - Close Value - Fee + Funding Fee
autoDeleverageTips: The current position in the auto deleverage sequence is represented by a solid bar. The more solid bars, the higher the likelihood of your position being automatically deleveraged.
stopTips: *Due to rapid market changes, the stop trigger price should not be too close to the estimated liquidation price to avoid failure to trigger
oraclePriceTooltip: The oracle price draws data from an external independent node service provider, which is used to calculate your liquidation price and position margin.
indexPriceTooltip: The Index Price is a composite price derived from the weighted average calculation of prices across multiple major exchanges.
closePositionTip: Your market close order may cause price fluctuations. Please confirm before proceeding with your trade.

perpDepositTips: Do not use keyless wallets to make deposits. Depositing with a keyless wallet may result in irreversible loss of funds.
perpAgreementTitle: By continuing, you agree to the Terms and Conditions and acknowledge that you have read and understood the Agreement Disclaimer, as well as the following:
perpAgreement1: I understand that if leverage is used, the leverage size may change after entering the position.
perpAgreement2: I understand the rules and risks of using margin when using cross margin mode.
perpAgreement3: I understand the rules and risks associated with profit and loss settlement, bankruptcy, insurance and loss sharing.
perpAgreement4: I understand that if my positions violate any margin maintenance requirements, my account may be partially or fully liquidated.
perpSignTips: You will receive 2 signature requests. Signing is free and does not trigger any transactions.
verifyOwnershipDesc: Confirm you are the owner of this wallet
enableTradingDesc: Enable secure access to our API for lightning quick trading
withdrawTips: Normal withdrawal is a two-step process. This step will initiate your withdrawal request, which will be processed in Layer2. Once the funds are ready (which may take up to 4 hours), you will receive a notification to withdraw funds to your wallet. Affected by chain synchronization and other circumstances, the time may be up to 48 hours. You are responsible for your own transaction gas fees.
crossWithdrawDesc: Cross-chain token withdrawals need to be verified at L2 (via zero-knowledge proof). The token withdrawal process takes up to about 2 hours. There must be sufficient funds in the asset pool of the relevant chain to handle cross-chain token withdrawals, during which mining fees will also be incurred. Ave will charge a certain handling fee as a mining fee.
marginRatioTips:
Margin Ratio
Margin Ratio = Maintenance Margin / Margin Balance. Your positions will be liquidated once Margin Ratio reaches 100%.
